title: ENHANCING ARTIFICIAL NEURAL NETWORKS WITH EPHAPTIC COUPLING
summary:
Systems and methods are disclosed for enhancing artificial neural networks using a computationally modeled ephaptic coupling mechanism to improve adaptability, efficiency, and learning performance. An example system includes a virtual modulation device configured to dynamically adjust one or more ephaptic coupling hyperparameters within an ephaptically coupled artificial neural network (EC-ANN) architecture. The modulation device operates via a Bayesian optimization agent within a closed feedback loop, enabling control over intra-layer field interactions. The virtual modulation device further includes a graphical user interface (GUI) for visualizing training metrics, configuring hyperparameters, and monitoring decision-making by the Bayesian optimization agent, with options for manual override and automated control. The virtual modulation device is integrated with a public key infrastructure and one or more hardware security modules to securely sign, deploy, and manage trained EC-ANN models. Secure elements embedded in deployment devices are used to enforce cryptographic authentication, lifecycle management, and revocation of deployed models.

ABSTRACT
Systems and methods are disclosed for enhancing artificial neural networks using a computationally modeled ephaptic coupling mechanism to improve adaptability, efficiency, and learning performance. An example system includes a virtual modulation device configured to dynamically adjust one or more ephaptic coupling hyperparameters within an ephaptically coupled artificial neural network (EC-ANN) architecture. The modulation device operates via a Bayesian optimization agent within a closed feedback loop, enabling control over intra-layer
field interactions. The virtual modulation device further includes a graphical user interface (GUI)
for visualizing training metrics, configuring hyperparameters, and monitoring decision-making by the Bayesian optimization agent, with options for manual override and automated control. The virtual modulation device is integrated with a public key infrastructure and one or more hardware security modules to securely sign, deploy, and manage trained EC-ANN models. Secure elements embedded in deployment devices are used to enforce cryptographic authentication, lifecycle management, and revocation of deployed models.

AMENDMENTS TO THE CLAIMSThe following listing of claims will replace all prior versions, and listings, of claims in the application.
1
1. (Currently Amended) A system comprising:one or more computers, each including at least one processor; and
one or more storage devices storing instructions that, when executed by the at least one processor, cause the system to implement, train, or use an artificial neural network that integrates an ephaptic coupling mechanism, wherein:
an artificial neuron in a hidden layer of the artificial neural network generates an output activation based on a combination of a synaptic input term and an ephaptic coupling term;
the synaptic input term is computed from a weighted combination of signals received from neurons in a preceding layer together with a corresponding bias;
the ephaptic coupling term is computed based on nonlinear transformations of post-synaptic activation states of other artificial neurons within the hidden layer, each transformation scaled by a corresponding ephaptic coupling coefficient that defines a strength of lateral influence between the artificial neuron and another artificial neuron within the hidden layer;
an ephaptic factor is applied to regulate an overall strength of the ephaptic coupling term; and
the ephaptic coupling mechanism is governed by a switchable, scalable, learnable, and tunable Ephaptic Coupling Matrix (ECM) engine initialized based on a transpose operation of a matrix of synaptic weights of at least one lay
er, that dynamically modifies activation states of artificial neurons through intra-layer interactions based on column-wise modulation, independently of and/or in conjunction with synaptic weights.

Currently amended

2
2. (Currently Amended) The system of claim 1, 
wherein the nonlinear transformations include one or more of: identity, rectified linear unit (ReLU), leaky rectified linear unit (leaky ReLU), sigmoid, hyperbolic tangent (tanh
), sigmoid-weighted linear unit (SiLU), Gaussian error linear unit (GELU
), or other continuous or piecewise-continuous nonlinear functions.

Currently amended

13. (Currently Amended) A computer-implemented method for operating an artificial neural network that integrates ephaptic coupling, the computer-implemented method comprising:initializing an Ephaptic Coupling Matrix (ECM) engine based on a transpose of a synaptic weight matrix of weights of at least a neural lay
er of the artificial neural network;

receiving one or more input signals into a into the neural layer of the artificial neural network; and
for each neuron in the neural layer:
computing a synaptic input term based on a weighted combination of signals from neurons in a preceding layer and an associated bias term,
retrieving a corresponding ephaptic coupling coefficient from the ECM engine,
computing an ephaptic coupling term based on a nonlinear transformation of activation states of other neurons within the neural layer, each activation state scaled by aby the corresponding ephaptic coupling coefficient;
combining the synaptic input term and the ephaptic coupling term to generate a total input signal for the neuron;
applying an activation function to the total input signal to produce an output activation for the neuron; and
propagating the output activation to a subsequent neural network layer or to an output layer for use in inference or training operations.

Currently amended

further comprising updating synaptic weights and/or ephaptic coupling coefficients using a learning mechanism selected from the group consisting of backpropagation, gradient-based optimization, reinforcement learning, Hebbian learning, and other biologically inspired or algorithmic learning mechanisms.
Original

, or other continuous or piecewise- continuous nonlinear functions.

19. (Withdrawn) A computer-implemented method using a virtual modulation device for securing an Ephaptically Coupled Artificial Neural Network (EC-ANN) deployment and inference comprising:generating a digital certificate associated with a trained artificial neural network model that integrates an ephaptic coupling mechanism;
cryptographically signing the trained artificial neural network model and its ephaptic modulation hyperparameters using a public key infrastructure linked to a first secure element embedded in a deployment device;
deploying the signed trained artificial neural network model to one or more computers comprising a second secure element configured to store cryptographic keys required for safe execution;
periodically authenticating the deployed artificial neural network model via communication with a remote governing authority over a wired or wireless network;
determining whether the deployed artificial neural network model is valid, expired, or flagged for revocation based on a result of cryptographic verification;
upon authentication failure, automatically disabling model execution by preventing intra- layer communication between artificial neurons of the artificial neural network model, thereby rendering the ephaptic coupling mechanism inoperable; and
implementing a cryptographic revocation mechanism that permanently disables the deployed artificial neural network model by rendering it incapable of future authentication or operation using said cryptographic keys.

Withdrawn

20
20. (Withdrawn) The computer-implemented method of claim 19, 
wherein lifecycles of digital certificates are managed by a public key infrastructure comprising one or more hardware security modules, governance of which is provided by a centralized or decentralized authority capable of issuing, renewing, or revoking certificates for secure artificial neural network deployment.

21. (Withdrawn) A method for deploying a kill switch feature of a deployment apparatus using a tamper-resistant, secure element with cryptographic keys, wherein the cryptographic keys are required for safe machine learning model execution, the method comprising:periodically authenticating a deployed artificial neural network instance via a remote governing authority over a wired or wireless network;
detecting whether the deployed artificial neural network instance is flagged for termination based on cryptographic verification;
automatically rendering the deployed artificial neural network instance inoperable by preventing interactions between neurons upon cryptographic authentication failure; and
implementing a cryptographic revocation system that permanently disables the deployed artificial neural network instance by preventing future authentication of said cryptographic keys.

Withdrawn

22

wherein the cryptographic revocation system can be either triggered remotely via a wired or wireless network or at an edge of a network via voice.
Withdrawn

wherein the secure element is a removable, embedded or integrated universal circuit card.
Withdrawn

24

wherein the secure element is a secure enclave or a trusted execution environment.
Withdrawn

25. (Withdrawn) The deployment apparatus of claim 21, 
further comprising a voice-based disabling mechanism operating at an edge of a network, wherein recognition of a predetermined password or phrase spoken by an authorized human user triggers modification or disabling operations of the deployed artificial neural network instance.

Withdrawn
